hypothesis: Augmenting the pharmacophore-guided generative framework with target-structure-derived 3D pharmacophore maps in the RL reward will produce molecules with at least a 1 kcal/mol improvement in median docking score and a ≥3-fold improvement in median in vitro binding affinity (IC50 or Kd) against the alpha estrogen receptor, while maintaining comparable scaffold novelty (mean Tanimoto ≤ 0.4 to the reference set) relative to molecules generated using only CATS-based pharmacophore similarity.
